I’m the kind of person who stubbornly refuses to do anything educational while trying to enjoy myself and relax. I’m the kind of person who typically enjoys fast paced, challenging games. I’m the kind of person who is a DIE HARD I Expect You To Die fan. This game genuinely changed me. I went into this game praying that Schell Games wouldn’t let me down, and boy oh boy, they delivered. They somehow made this a PERFECT combination of learning and fun. I actually enjoyed learning about the different cultures and foods that it presented to me. My one and only complaint is that I could use some more recipes, I finished the game relatively quickly, but I’m working on going back and getting 5 stars on all the recipes. I’ve never played a bad game from Schell, and they did not disappoint. Highly recommend!

Great game, but do wish they would update it with more locations and recipes. So much that could be added to make this even more amazing. Free play mode would also be awesome! I hope the developers listen to us and choose to add on to this game.

reviews.review_title: Nice but I am hungry for more!
reviews.review_description: I love this game! It's detailed, immersive, and fun, with diverse cultures, foods, and guided recipes. The narrators are great, and the environments are beautiful. However, after mastering the 9 initial recipes, the game feels short. I’d love more recipes and a feature where I can experiment with all the ingredients in a kitchen. I'm excited for the promised updates—when can we expect more content?
It’s so exciting to actually cook old time dishes but it’s way too short and should have been more dishes for this paid app

reviews.review_title: Fair price, but wanting more recipes
reviews.review_description: I don't mind the prince, $15 seems fair to pay for this game, but if there's ever a chance to where there would be some added on content I would love to see more recipes and cultures!
Really relaxing play through. A bit short but it’s worth my money

This game is awesome! I haven’t even finished the game yet and I’m enjoying every second of it. I highly recommend this game, it’s worth the price, and it’s relaxing. I like how you are not timed when you are making the food, it actually lets you take as much time as you want which it excellent. The graphics and the way the game looks is amazing and I couldn’t believe on how they used real people!! That’s insane but really cool! I hope this review helps anyone that is debating whether to buy it or not! :)

I really love the idea of cooking sims on VR, especially true cooking sims that you can apply your knowledge in the kitchen after mastering a mission. I love the respect they showed each culture even taking the time to cross reference with historical specialists to guarantee realism.
There is a tremendous amount of detail put into the concept and execution here. Everything has a little bug here or there on launch day. So I have no qualms with things like translucent pork or 2D liquid animations. At the end of the day they have taken a novel idea done a great job in making it truly immersive and fun. And even put all the recipes in pdf format on their website!
Long story short, this was a great game with tons of love and respect toward the traditions and historical accuracy portrayed. My only hope is that there is more to come. I would love to see some more cultures through DLC and expansions. I can almost guarantee I would have to collect them all!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, we're so glad to hear you're enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description:
Fun design and game play. I think more could be done with the scoring system such as 'cleanliness' using hands to stir etc. to differentiate 1-5 stars or make it challenging.

With only 9 recipes it definitely left me wanting more!

I've been waiting for this since I saw the preview. I really love it but only giving it 4 stars because sometimes you need extra ingredients if you mess up a little and it only gives you the exact amount. And also sometimes ingredients just vanish off the table lol. But aside from that it's really fun.
